---
title: "Lecture 19: ES6+ Features"
subtitle: "{{< meta course.number >}} - {{< meta course.title >}}"
author: "{{< meta instructor.name >}}"
date: "February 25, 2026"
code-annotations: below
---


## {#title-slide data-menu-title="Title Slide" background="#053660"}

<div class="custom-title">
[Lecture 19]{.baby-blue-text}
</div>

<div class="custom-subtitle">
[ES6+ Features]{.white-text}
</div>

<div class="custom-subtitle3">
Week 5 | Unit II: DOM Manipulation<br>
{{< meta course.number >}} - {{< meta course.title >}}<br>
{{< meta instructor.name >}}, {{< meta instructor.title >}}
</div>

---

## {data-menu-title="Learning Objectives"}

[Learning Objectives]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
- Use template literals for string interpolation
- Destructure arrays and objects
- Apply the spread operator effectively
- Set default function parameters
- Understand `let` and `const` scope rules
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="What is ES6"}

[What is ES6+?]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
**ES6** (ECMAScript 2015) introduced major improvements to JavaScript:

- Cleaner syntax
- Better scoping with `let` and `const`
- Powerful new features (spread, destructuring)
- Modern coding patterns
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Template Literals"}

[Template Literals]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1-2|3|4"}
const name = "Priya";
const age = 22;
const message = `Hello, ${name}! You are ${age} years old.`;
console.log(message);
```

::: {.body-text-m}
Use backticks (`` ` ``) and `${}` for dynamic values.
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Multi-line Strings"}

[Multi-line Strings]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1-4"}
const poem = `Roses are red,
Violets are blue,
JavaScript rocks,
And so do you!`;
console.log(poem);
```

No need for `\n` or concatenation.

---

## {data-menu-title="let and const"}

[let and const]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1-2|4-5|7-8"}
// let: reassignable
let score = 50;
score = 60; // OK

// const: not reassignable
const pi = 3.14;
pi = 3.15; // Error

const user = { name: "Aman" };
user.name = "Vikram"; // OK (object mutation allowed)
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Block Scope"}

[Block Scope with let/const]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1-3|4"}
if (true) {
  let x = 10;
}
console.log(x); // Error: x not defined
```

::: {.body-text-m}
`let` and `const` are block-scoped, unlike `var`.
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Default Parameters"}

[Default Function Parameters]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2|5|6"}
function greet(name = "Guest") {
  return `Hello, ${name}`;
}

console.log(greet());        // Hello, Guest
console.log(greet("Neha"));  // Hello, Neha
```

Provide fallback values when arguments are missing.

---

## {data-menu-title="Array Destructuring"}

[Array Destructuring]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2|3|5|6"}
const colors = ['red', 'green', 'blue'];
const [first, second, third] = colors;
console.log(first); // red

const [a, , c] = colors;
console.log(a, c); // red blue
```

Extract values from arrays into variables.

---

## {data-menu-title="Object Destructuring"}

[Object Destructuring]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2|3|5|6"}
const student = { name: "Asha", age: 21, course: "CSE" };
const { name, age } = student;
console.log(name, age); // Asha 21

const { name: studentName, age: studentAge } = student;
console.log(studentName); // Asha
```

Extract properties from objects.

---

## {data-menu-title="Spread Operator Arrays"}

[Spread Operator: Arrays]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2|3|5|6"}
const arr1 = [1, 2, 3];
const arr2 = [...arr1, 4, 5];
console.log(arr2); // [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]

const combined = [...arr1, ...arr2];
console.log(combined); // [1, 2, 3, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
```

Spread (`...`) expands array elements.

---

## {data-menu-title="Spread Operator Objects"}

[Spread Operator: Objects]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2|3|5|6"}
const user = { name: "Raj", age: 25 };
const updatedUser = { ...user, age: 26 };
console.log(updatedUser); // { name: "Raj", age: 26 }

const merged = { ...user, city: "Delhi" };
console.log(merged); // { name: "Raj", age: 25, city: "Delhi" }
```

Copy and merge objects easily.

---

## {data-menu-title="Rest Parameters"}

[Rest Parameters]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2|5"}
function sum(...numbers) {
  return numbers.reduce((acc, n) => acc + n, 0);
}

console.log(sum(1, 2, 3, 4)); // 10
```

::: {.body-text-m}
Rest (`...`) collects arguments into an array.
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Arrow + Destructure"}

[Combining Arrow Functions & Destructuring]{.slide-title3}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2|4-5"}
const users = [{ name: "Amit", age: 22 }, { name: "Sita", age: 24 }];
const names = users.map(({ name }) => name);
console.log(names); // ["Amit", "Sita"]

const greet = ({ name, age }) => `${name} is ${age} years old`;
console.log(greet(users[0])); // Amit is 22 years old
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Ternary"}

[Ternary Operator Refresher]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2"}
const age = 20;
const status = age >= 18 ? "Adult" : "Minor";
console.log(status); // Adult
```

::: {.body-text-m}
Shorthand for `if...else`.
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Short Circuit"}

[Short-circuit Evaluation]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2|4|5"}
const username = user.name || "Guest";
const display = isLoggedIn && <Dashboard />;

const firstName = user?.name?.first;
const count = items?.length ?? 0;
```

::: {.body-text-m}
- `||` returns first truthy value
- `&&` returns second if first is truthy
- `?.` optional chaining
- `??` nullish coalescing
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Modules"}

[ES6 Modules (Preview)]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1-2|6"}
// math.js
export const add = (a, b) => a + b;
export const subtract = (a, b) => a - b;

// app.js
import { add, subtract } from './math.js';
```

::: {.body-text-m}
Organize code into reusable modules.
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Practice Example"}

[Practice Example]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1-4|6|7|8"}
const students = [
  { name: "Rahul", marks: 85 },
  { name: "Neha", marks: 92 }
];

const toppers = students.filter(({ marks }) => marks > 90);
const names = toppers.map(({ name }) => name);
console.log(names); // ["Neha"]
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Common Mistakes"}

[Common Mistakes]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
- Using `var` instead of `let`/`const`
- Forgetting backticks in template literals
- Modifying `const` primitive values
- Confusing spread `...` with rest `...`
- Not understanding block scope
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Practice Task"}

[Practice Task]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
Given:
```javascript
const products = [
  { name: "Laptop", price: 50000 },
  { name: "Phone", price: 20000 }
];
```

1. Use destructuring to extract `name` and `price`
2. Use template literals to display: "Laptop costs ₹50000"
3. Use spread to add a new product
:::

---

## {#end-slide data-menu-title="End Slide" background="#053660"}

<div class="page-center">
<div class="custom-subtitle">
[Questions?]{.white-text}
</div>
<div class="custom-subtitle3">
Next: Lecture 20 - Revision: Units I & II
</div>
</div>
